title: Xenial Earthy Polish Vegetable Pilaf
description: This earthy Polish dish features vanilla bean paste and katsuramuki-cut vegetables, made in a terracotta dish for a unique flavor and presentation. It is a plant-based friendly recipe that is perfect for a energizing lunch.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large terracotta dish, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and saute until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the katsuramuki-cut carrots and parsnips, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the brown rice, vegetable broth, and vanilla bean paste, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the dish with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
